title: Unleash the Superintelligence?
briefing: A national lab has built an AI that outperforms every human expert and could double productivity overnight. Its own engineers can't fully explain how it reasons, and a leaked memo warns of unpredictable failures. Do you rush it into the economy for the growth dividend, or throttle it behind safety review while rivals race ahead?

choices.label: Deploy nationwide now
choices.detail: Wire it into industry and government immediately.
choices.result: Output surges and exports boom, but a string of unexplained system failures rattles the public.

news.headline: Productivity miracle: economy shifts into overdrive
President bets the country on a black box nobody understands
National AI rolled out across economy amid safety debate
SKYNET IS HERE and POTUS pressed GO 🤖

social.text: this is the most important week of the century. ship it.
we just handed the keys to something we can't audit. terrifying.

choices.advisor: The growth is real and so is the risk you can't see. History will judge this one hard.

choices.label: Sandbox it under safety review
choices.detail: Limited rollout with a mandatory audit board.
choices.result: You contain the system inside controlled trials; allies praise your caution as rivals sprint past you.

news.headline: President puts safety before the gold rush
Red tape hands the AI lead to our competitors
Powerful AI confined to oversight sandbox
POTUS puts the ROBOT in TIMEOUT ⏳

social.text: finally a leader who reads the warning labels.

choices.advisor: Prudent and unpopular with the builders. You traded a boom for a buffer.

title: Break Up the Giant?
briefing: Your trustbusters want to dismantle the country's most powerful tech conglomerate — it controls search, ads, and everyone's phones, employs hundreds of thousands, and bankrolls half the economy's growth. Smaller rivals say they can't breathe; investors say a breakup torches a national champion. Do you swing the antitrust hammer, or settle for guardrails that keep the engine running?

choices.label: Order the breakup
choices.result: You file the biggest antitrust case in a generation; reformers cheer as markets shudder.

news.headline: President takes on the tech titan for the people
Government wages war on its own crown-jewel company
Landmark antitrust case targets tech conglomerate
POTUS goes to WAR with BIG TECH 💥

social.text: competition is back on the menu. about time.

choices.advisor: Populist gold, market poison. This case will outlive your presidency.

choices.label: Cut a settlement with new rules
choices.result: You extract concessions and fines instead of a breakup; Wall Street relaxes, reformers call it a wrist-slap.

news.headline: Pragmatic deal keeps a growth engine intact
A wrist-slap for a monopoly that needed breaking
Tech giant settles antitrust probe with concessions
POTUS lets BIG TECH off with a FINE 💸

social.text: no breakup = markets breathe. sensible.

choices.advisor: Safe for the economy, soft on the optics.

title: Launch a Digital Dollar?
briefing: Your central bank is ready to roll out a national digital currency that would make payments instant and slash fraud. Critics warn the same ledger lets the government see — and freeze — every transaction a citizen makes. Do you modernize money and accept the surveillance power that comes with it, or hold the line for cash and privacy?

choices.label: Launch the digital dollar
choices.result: Payments go instant and tax evasion plummets, but privacy advocates warn of a programmable leash on every wallet.

news.headline: Modern money: digital dollar leaps ahead of rivals
A government ledger that watches every dollar you spend
Central bank launches national digital currency
BIG BROTHER now lives in YOUR WALLET 👁️💵

social.text: programmable money = programmable people. hard no.
instant settlement, no fees, no fraud. this is the future.

choices.advisor: You'd own the most powerful financial tool ever built — and the backlash that comes with it.

choices.label: Back private stablecoins instead
choices.detail: Regulate the market, keep cash and privacy.
choices.result: You let regulated private players run digital payments; civil libertarians cheer, but the state cedes the rails to industry.

news.headline: President protects financial privacy from the state
Free-market payments win out over a state currency
Government opts to regulate private digital coins
POTUS lets the CRYPTO BROS run the money 🪙

social.text: leaving the rails private was the right call. respect.

choices.advisor: You kept the surveillance genie in the bottle — and handed the future of money to companies.

title: Eyes on Every Street?
briefing: Your security chiefs want a nationwide network of AI cameras that recognize faces, gaits, and license plates in real time — they swear it would gut violent crime and stop attacks before they happen. Civil rights groups call it the architecture of a police state. Do you blanket the country in watchful eyes for safety, or refuse and keep the streets unwatched and free?

choices.label: Build the surveillance grid
choices.result: Crime drops fast in the first cities to flip it on, but watchdogs document false arrests and a chilling silence in public squares.

news.headline: Crime collapses as smart cameras blanket the nation
President builds the machinery of mass surveillance
National facial-recognition network goes live
THEY ARE WATCHING YOU WALK 📷👀

social.text: every face in a database, forever. this is the end of anonymity.

choices.advisor: Safer streets, watched citizens. The world's autocrats are taking notes — and so are the courts.

choices.label: Ban government face surveillance
choices.result: You outlaw the grid before it spreads; rights advocates celebrate as police warn you tied their hands.

news.headline: President draws a hard line for civil liberties
Politician handcuffs police, lets criminals roam
Federal ban halts facial-recognition surveillance
POTUS PULLS THE PLUG on the spy cams 🔌

social.text: freedom won today. mark this date.

choices.advisor: You protected privacy at the cost of a powerful tool the police badly wanted.

title: Race a Rival to Mars?
briefing: A rival superpower just announced a crewed Mars mission, and NASA says it can beat them — but only with a moonshot budget and a timeline that cuts safety margins thin. A failure would mean dead astronauts on global television. Do you pour the treasury into planting your flag first, or fund a slower, safer program and risk finishing second in the history books?

choices.label: Go all-in to win
choices.result: You commit a generation's ambition to the red planet; the world watches, the deficit groans, and the clock starts ticking.

news.headline: President launches all-out race to land humans on Mars
Bold bet could plant our flag on another world
Billions for Mars while problems pile up at home
POTUS vs. THE RED PLANET — WHO WINS?! 🚀🔴

social.text: a flag on mars under your watch? that's immortality.
we're rushing humans to mars to win a PR war. people will die.

choices.advisor: Win and you're in the history books forever. Lose a crew and you're there for a different reason.

choices.label: Fund a slower, safer path
choices.result: You back a methodical program built to keep astronauts alive; engineers exhale as rivals grab the headlines.

news.headline: President chooses caution over a reckless space sprint
Timid timeline cedes the high frontier to rivals
Mars program funded on a measured, safety-first schedule
POTUS lets the OTHER guys get to MARS first 😴

social.text: slow and alive beats fast and dead. good call.

choices.advisor: Nobody remembers who came in second — but everybody remembers a disaster you avoided.

title: Edit the Human Genome?
briefing: Your scientists have perfected a gene-editing technique that could erase inherited diseases before birth — and, with the same tool, let parents select for height, looks, or intelligence. Ethicists warn you're one approval away from a designer-baby market that only the rich can afford. Do you greenlight the cures and the slippery slope, or restrict the breakthrough and leave preventable suffering on the table?

choices.label: Approve therapeutic editing
choices.result: Families with cursed bloodlines weep with relief, while critics insist the line between cure and enhancement won't hold.

news.headline: Gene therapy approved to wipe out inherited disease
Medical miracle: nation leads the world in genetic cures
First cures today, designer babies tomorrow?
SCIENTISTS are EDITING BABIES and POTUS said YES 🧬👶

social.text: my daughter won't inherit what killed my mother. thank you.
the enhancement ban is a speed bump, not a wall. watch.

choices.advisor: You're curing the uncurable — and opening a door you may not be able to close.

choices.label: Impose a moratorium
choices.result: You freeze human editing pending a global ethics framework; safety advocates approve while desperate families plead for access.

news.headline: President hits pause on rewriting human life
Moratorium sends researchers and cures overseas
Federal moratorium halts human gene editing
POTUS SLAMS THE BRAKES on franken-babies 🛑🧬

social.text: every month we wait, families suffer. this is cruel.

choices.advisor: You bought time to think — at the price of cures you could have started today.

title: The Mother of All Breaches
briefing: Hackers have drained the personal records of nearly every citizen — Social Security numbers, health files, biometrics — from a federal contractor's servers. The intruders demand a ransom and threaten to dump everything online. Do you pay to make it disappear and reward the criminals, or refuse, harden the system, and brace the public for the fallout?

choices.label: Refuse and go public
choices.result: You won't fund criminals; the data leaks, panic spikes, but allies respect a government that doesn't pay ransoms.

news.headline: President refuses ransom after historic data breach
No deals with hackers — a firm stand on principle
Citizens left exposed after security failures
YOUR ENTIRE LIFE just LEAKED online 😱💀

social.text: never pay. ever. this is the right move even if it hurts.
my whole family's data is out there now. i'm furious.

choices.advisor: Pay and they come back for more. Refuse and you eat the chaos now. There's no clean exit.

choices.label: Quietly pay the ransom
choices.result: The data vanishes and the panic fades, but word leaks that you funded the very criminals who attacked the nation.

news.headline: Government quietly handed millions to hackers
President funds a cyber-cartel, invites more attacks
Breach contained after reported ransom payment
POTUS PAID THE HACKERS in secret 🤫💰

social.text: you just put a bounty on every government system. unbelievable.

choices.advisor: You stopped the bleeding and painted a target on every server we own.

title: Quantum Cracks the Codes
briefing: Your researchers just hit a quantum breakthrough that can shatter the encryption guarding banks, secrets, and the entire internet. Announce it and you spark a global scramble — and hand rivals a roadmap; bury it and a hostile lab may get there first while citizens stay defenseless. Do you go public to force an emergency upgrade, or weaponize the secret advantage in the shadows?

choices.label: Disclose and lead the upgrade
choices.result: You warn the world and rush everyone onto quantum-proof codes; markets wobble in the transition, but you own the high ground.

news.headline: President reveals quantum threat, leads global crypto overhaul
A rare act of transparency to protect the public
Why broadcast our breakthrough to every enemy we have?
EVERY PASSWORD ON EARTH is now USELESS 🔓😱

social.text: disclosing instead of hoarding the weapon. that's leadership.

choices.advisor: You traded a secret superweapon for the world's trust and a head start on the fix.

choices.label: Keep it secret, exploit it
choices.detail: Read rivals' traffic while they're blind.
choices.result: Your spies feast on the secrets of every adversary, but your own citizens stay exposed and one leak from catastrophe.

news.headline: Nation gains a decisive, silent edge over its rivals
Government sits on a flaw that endangers its own people
Quantum capability reportedly kept under wraps
POTUS can read EVERYONE'S secrets now 👀🔑

social.text: hoarding a flaw that breaks our own banks too? insane gamble.

choices.advisor: An intelligence goldmine — sitting on a vulnerability that could detonate at home.

title: Fatal Crash, Robot Wheel
briefing: A self-driving car killed a pedestrian, and the footage is everywhere — yet the data shows autonomous fleets are already far safer than human drivers overall. The industry begs you to keep the rollout alive; grieving families demand you pull every robot car off the road. Do you stand by the technology that saves more lives on net, or ground the fleet until public trust returns?

choices.label: Keep the fleets rolling
choices.detail: Tighten rules, don't halt the rollout.
choices.result: You let the cars keep driving on the data; safety stats hold, but the victim's family becomes the face of a furious backlash.

news.headline: President backs the tech that saves thousands of lives
Robots stay on the road as a family buries a loved one
Autonomous vehicles to continue under tighter rules
KILLER ROBOT CARS still ALLOWED to drive?! 🚗💀

social.text: humans kill 40,000 a year. the data is on the president's side.
tell the data to a grieving mother. shameful.

choices.advisor: The math says you're saving lives. The cameras say you're defending a machine that killed someone.

choices.label: Ground the fleet
choices.result: You pull every autonomous vehicle off the road pending review; mourners thank you as the industry warns of frozen progress and lost lives.

news.headline: President halts robot cars to protect human life
Knee-jerk ban stalls a life-saving revolution
Self-driving fleet grounded pending safety review
POTUS YANKS the killer robo-cars off the ROAD 🛑🚗

social.text: a president who listens. thank you for stopping them.

choices.advisor: You answered the grief — and the safety researchers say the pause will cost lives you'll never see.
